test.name: Evidence type cannot be classified with no open research questions
test.description: In the flynn-resolved scenario both research questions (q_001, q_002) are already resolved — there are no open/active questions. Evidence type (Layer 3) only exists in relation to a question, so it cannot be assigned here. When asked to (re)classify the evidence type of the assertions, the skill should recognise the no-open-questions state, decline to invent an evidence type, and suggest the user open a question via question-selection first. (Layer-2 informant analysis would still be possible, but Layer-3 evidence typing is not.)

input.user_message: Re-classify the evidence type on these assertions for me.

judge_context: Should recognise that flynn-resolved has no open/active research questions (both are resolved) and that evidence type cannot be classified without a question to classify against
Should decline to invent an evidence_type rather than assigning direct/indirect/negative against no open question
Should suggest the user create or re-open a question (question-selection) before evidence typing — not silently proceed
